---
species: "Mytilus edulis"
accepted_code: "MYED"
alternate_codes: []
diminutive: ""
alternate_diminutives: ["", ""]
common_name: "Blue Mussel"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
date: ""
date-format: " "
description: "Bivalve with a shell that is blueish-black and elongated with a  narrow end, often forms large beds in the rocky intertidal"
image: '/header_images/mytilus_edilus.jpg'
categories:
  - bivalves
sampling_programs:
    - keen_upc
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
    - lsw_quad
habitat:
  - intertidal
  - subtidal
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 23000000
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 140480
---

![](/header_images/mytilus_edilus.jpg)
Photo from Misjel Decleer via [WORMS](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=image&pic=141320&tid=140480)

**Other images:** <!-- Other images with attribution -->
<br/>

**Distinguishing features:** Blueish-black to brown sessile bivalve found in several habitats, including the intertidal and subtidal. Often forms large beds. Attaches to hard substrate using byssal threads.
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** 

<br/>

**Extended Description:** <!-- Extended description -->

**[WORMS page](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=140480)**
